title: Graph-based Prediction and Planning Policy Network (GP3Net) for scalable self-driving in dynamic environments using Deep Reinforcement Learning

methodology.name: Graph-based Prediction and Planning Policy Network (GP3Net)
methodology.problem_addressed: Motion planning for autonomous vehicles (AVs) in non-stationary and dynamic environments.

categories.description: Uses predefined rules for decision-making, such as at intersections.
categories.limitation: Suffers from scalability issues due to the non-stationary nature of environments.

categories.description: Learns driving behaviors by mimicking expert human drivers or simulator data.
categories.limitation: Suffers from data distribution shift from training scenarios to unseen evaluation scenarios.

categories.description: Not detailed in the provided text, but mentioned as a category of state-of-the-art methods.

motivation.problem: Ensuring safety and comfort for AVs in complex, real-world driving environments with multiple traffic participants (cars, bikers, pedestrians) and varying conditions (weather, speeds).
challenges: Modeling the different intentions and goals of other traffic participants.
Handling variable speeds, which are a significant cause of crashes (responsible for 8.4% of critical crash reasons).
Dealing with unstructured environments with cooperative/non-cooperative behaviors.
Overcoming limitations of communication-based solutions (different protocols, real-time reliability issues).
Addressing the shortcomings of existing methods like rule-based systems (scalability) and imitation learning (data distribution shift).

evaluation.details: The provided text includes the introduction and methodology setup but does not contain any results, experimental data, or performance metrics.
